subject: Re: Removing bad tilts
body:
Thank you for the quick response Alister. I wanted to make sure I am not
missing anything here, the script (with some help from github copilot)
turned out to be easier than I thought!

https://github.com/hamid13r/warp_lamella_adapters/blob/main/remove_bad_views.py

After this I just ran ts_reconstruct and the "shadows" of bad views seem to
be gone!

Best,
Hamid

On Mon, Oct 6, 2025 at 12:32 PM Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hey Hamid,
>
> Tilt deselection is not handled particularly elegantly in WarpTools (no
> GUI to make it easy) - the GUI is incoming but no firm timeline to share
> yet, until then: script!
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>
> On Oct 6, 2025, at 11:16, Hamidreza Rahmani <hamid13r@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> ﻿
> Hi all,
>
> I have a dataset that I aligned in etomo and to get a better model/remove
> junks I removed some tiles in the fine alignment step (using 1,28-36 so
> there are a bunch of bad tilts).
>
> When I use ts_import_alignment I see all the views are imported into the
> xml files and the use tilt section is True for all of them.
>
> Is there an easy way to fix this?
>
> Do you recommend I write a code to look at taSolution.log and edit the xml
> files to change those views to False in use Tilt section?
>
> Best,
> Hamid
>
> --

> .
>
>
